description:
You are working as a junior log-analyst who wants to try the “loguru” Python
    library for better console logging.  
    Your goal is to do a very small package-management check:

    1. Install the exact PyPI package version “loguru==0.7.0” for your **current
       user only** (do NOT attempt a system-wide installation that needs sudo).
       Use any reasonable Python/pip invocation you like, as long as the
       installation is successful for the account “user”.

    2. Immediately verify the installation by importing the package and printing
       its `__version__` attribute.

    3. Redirect that one-line version string into the text file
       “/home/user/log_install_report.txt”.

    Output format that the automatic grader will check:
    • The file “/home/user/log_install_report.txt” must exist.
    • It must contain exactly one line (terminated with a single newline) that
      looks like this – note the lowercase “loguru” and the single space after
      the colon:

        loguru version: 0.7.0

      (No extra spaces before/after the line, no additional lines.)

    The entire task can be accomplished with roughly two or three terminal
    commands: one to install, one to run Python and redirect the output
    (combining import + print), and optionally one more to double-check or view
    the report.  That is all the grader will validate.
truth:
The grader will:
    1. Open /home/user/log_install_report.txt.
    2. Assert that its exact byte contents are:
       "loguru version: 0.7.0\n"
    3. Launch “python3 - <<'PY'\nimport loguru, sys; print(loguru.__version__)\nPY”
       and expect “0.7.0” on stdout (plus a trailing newline).
name: task_000000_31052eaa
